spec_match.rationale: TOC + article with `scroll-target-group: auto` is the canonical use case for the spec.

interactive.rationale: Zero interactive markers — only browser scroll. Invariant 7 forbids static demos.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb correctly describes the no-JS active-section UX but the page itself has nothing the visitor can drive beyond scrolling.

edge_cases.rationale: No demonstration of the JS-driven alternative for comparison, no toggle to disable group, no demonstration of long article + sticky TOC interactions.

references.rationale: No chromestatus.com/feature link on page — invariant 3 violation.

openQuestions.title: Add toggle vs IntersectionObserver alternative
openQuestions.detail: Two TOCs side-by-side: one with scroll-target-group, one with traditional IntersectionObserver JS. Visitor scrolls and sees both update — proves the no-JS angle is real.

openQuestions.title: Sticky TOC interactions
openQuestions.detail: TOC + position: sticky is common. Show how the active highlight behaves when TOC is pinned vs scrolling with the article.

summary: Same shape as anchor-carousel: right idea, ships as static. The TOC vs IntersectionObserver comparison is the obvious interactive angle and would land the value proposition hard.
